name: plan_manifest_wave1_runtime_owner
description: Add the runtime owner for raw control-plane manifests backed by app_manifests history.

jobs.label: add_control_plane_manifest_runtime_owner
jobs.prompt:
## Goal
Create a runtime owner for registry-first control-plane manifests so data plans and approvals stop pretending to be file artifacts.

## Read first
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/artifacts/workflow/_control_manifest_registry_contract.json`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/canonical_manifests.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/workflow_runtime_repository.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/helm_manifest.py`

## Build
Create a new module:
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_plane_manifests.py`

It should provide helpers for raw control-plane manifest ownership WITHOUT forcing them through helm normalization. Use the existing app-manifest tables as the durable backend.

Implement helpers for:
- creating a data plan manifest
- creating a data approval manifest
- loading a control-plane manifest by id
- extracting the plan payload from a plan manifest
- extracting the approval payload from an approval manifest
- updating plan status transitions such as `draft -> approved -> applied`

## Rules
- The raw manifest payload must keep `kind=\"praxis_control_manifest\"` and `manifest_family=\"control_plane\"`.
- Reject manifests that do not match the expected family/type.
- Record history through `app_manifest_history` when mutating current-state rows.
- Do not touch API handlers or data-plane runtime in this packet.
- Do not use helm bundle normalization.

## Required tests
Create:
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_control_plane_manifests.py`

Cover at least:
- create plan manifest shapes the raw payload correctly
- create approval manifest links back to the parent plan manifest
- load rejects wrong manifest families/kinds
- status transition updates call through storage owner correctly

Database: `$WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL`
